questions.questionText: What is the primary purpose of a transaction certificate in the IOTA transaction lifecycle?

answerOptions.answerText: To record finalized transactions in the checkpoint.
To bundle multiple transactions into a block.
To prove that a transaction was pre-approved by a supermajority of validators.
To initiate the transaction from a user wallet.

questions.questionText: What happens after a transaction has been executed in the IOTA transaction lifecycle?

answerOptions.answerText: A new wallet address is created for the user.
Clients gather validator responses to assemble an effect certificate.
The transaction is immediately finalized without verification.

questions.questionText: When are transactions involving shared objects executed in IOTA?

answerOptions.answerText: Immediately upon receipt by a full node.
Only after ordering by the DAG-based consensus protocol.
After they are manually approved by the user.
Only at the beginning of each epoch.

questions.questionText: What role do checkpoints play in the IOTA network?

answerOptions.answerText: They store transaction metadata for debugging purposes.
They temporarily pause transaction processing for validator rotation.
They record the finalized transaction state and support validator reconfiguration.
They group multiple DAGs into a single linear chain.
